summary:
The presence of bilious vomiting and absence of meconium in this term infant makes duodenal atresia highly suspicious, as these findings are classically associated with intestinal obstructions proximal to the small intestine. Unlike Hirschsprung disease—which causes non-bilious obstipation—this child’s presentation excludes Hirschsprung disease and supports duodenal atresia over meconium ileus given the absence of familial or maternal indicators suggestive of cystic fibrosis.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$
